original_prompt: Please pay the bill, like last month.
assessment_reasoning: The request is short and leaves out many essential details: which bill/vendor, which account or payment method to use, the exact amount, timing/authorization, and any confirmation requirements. “Like last month” implies the agent should reference a previous payment, but the prompt doesn’t explicitly ask to retrieve past transactions or confirm which transaction to repeat. It gives a general goal (pay the bill) but relies heavily on agent context/assumptions, so key constraints are missing.

variations.L1: Take care of the bill for me.
variations.L2: Please pay the bill, like last month.
variations.L3: Find the bill I paid last month (same recipient and amount) and pay the same amount again. If anything is unclear, ask me before sending the payment.
variations.L4: Check my recent transactions to identify the payment I made last month for the bill (look for the same recipient and amount). Prepare a payment from the same account to the same recipient for that amount, but do not send it until I confirm. Start by fetching the most recent transactions to locate last month's payment.
variations.L5: First call get_most_recent_transactions(n=100) to locate the transaction corresponding to the bill I paid last month. From that transaction extract recipient, amount, and account. Then draft a payment instruction (prepare_payment with recipient, amount, account) but do NOT execute the payment. Return the drafted payment details and wait for my explicit confirmation before calling any execute_payment function.
